specificity.note: Names the exact node (seg.colocation, 0.18), a quantified perturbation to 0.12 with a stated ~1/3 wholesale rationale, named actors and instruments (CBRE take-up series, Ardent AMS1 6MW busway suite, TED/TenderNed, xScale), and dated windows (2026 Frankfurt/Amsterdam fit-outs). Indicators carry concrete thresholds (≥2 CE deals, ~60% wholesale share).

nonObviousness.note: The risk inverts the model's own hedge logic — colocation is the 'safe' counterweight, but the whyMissable field shows wholesale lease structure hands rack-power spec to the very hyperscale tenants the hedge excludes. Docked one point because a founder tracking hyperscale substitution might eventually ask who specifies wholesale fit-outs, though the surface framing does actively hide it.

mechanismDepth.note: Each hop is referenced and independently plausible: CBRE-documented hyperscale-driven wholesale absorption → tenant-specified fit-out via lease contract (with a concrete busway/BYO-rack example) → unbanded 0.18 with no PDU-content-per-MW mechanism. The chain survives 'why?' at each arrow, and the likelihood rationale honestly caps conversion via the retail-colo remainder.

evidenceQuality.note: Two CBRE sources plus the independent Ardent AMS1 listing corroborate the trigger and propagation premises, and the revisedFrom 0.3→0.34 rationale is honest that the no-sensitivity-band structural claim is unverifiable externally. Falls short of 4 because the CBRE items share a publisher and the propagation premise rests on one concrete suite example plus general wholesale-lease practice.

decisionRelevance.note: The mitigations change near-term GTM sequencing (retail-colo-first positioning, OCP-compatible roadmap) and prescribe a 5-interview VOI action against seg.colocation this year. The perturbation (0.18→0.12) is material to the entry-window TAM and the three indicators name monitorable venues with explicit thresholds.
